name: Fight Colorectal Cancer
mission: Learn what colorectal cancer is, how it develops, and the key facts, symptoms, and screening guidelines to know.
theoryOfChange: Fight Colorectal Cancer has a published strategic framework called the Colorectal Cancer Care Initiative (CRCCI), detailed in their Colorectal Cancer Care Report. This initiative is a national coalition uniting patient advocates, physicians, public health champions, and healthcare leaders around data-driven goals to transform colorectal cancer screening, diagnosis, and treatment. The framework establishes clear, actionable goals, such as achieving an 80% screening rate for average-risk patients and ensuring 80% of patients with an abnormal non-invasive screening test receive a follow-up colonoscopy within 90 days. It also aims for 80% of patients to initiate treatment within six weeks of diagnosis, and for 80% of patients diagnosed with stage III or IV CRC to receive biomarker and genetic testing. The report utilizes real-world data, scientific literature, and patient experience to set these goals for improving prevention, diagnosis, and care nationwide.
scoreSummary: Fight Colorectal Cancer shows strong impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the cause addresses a critical global health urgency, the alignment for Muslim donors is considered medium-low (30/50) because the organization lacks specific religious giving pathways or targeted outreach to the community [8]. Contributions are categorized as Sadaqah-eligible, serving as general voluntary charity for public health advancement [8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high effectiveness in resource allocation, maintaining a program expense ratio of 85.2% [1][2]. Its impact is primarily driven through capacity building, public awareness, and research grants aimed at long-term health outcomes [7]. With 12.0 months of working capital, the charity maintains a stable financial foundation to sustain its advocacy and education programs [5].

baseline_narrative.headline: Fight Colorectal Cancer empowers a global community of survivors and advocates to advance medical research and public policy.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 85.2%, ensuring the majority of funds reach core services
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.07 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity position with 12.0 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: Fight Colorectal Cancer is a national advocacy organization dedicated to supporting patients through education, awareness, and legislative action [1]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $3,060,301 to fund its mission of finding a cure and supporting those affected by the disease [2][5].

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has a documented theory of change and tracks outcome metrics via Candid, though independent RCT-level evaluations are not present.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Fight CRC uses advocacy and education to influence policy and research funding, ultimately aiming to reduce colorectal cancer mortality.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts are effectively translating into policy changes and research advancements.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: As a $3M organization in a major health space, they have significant capacity to scale advocacy and research grants.

strengths.detail: The charity maintains a <cite id="2">program expense ratio of 85.2%</cite>, which exceeds the <cite id="1">78.7% peer program ratio median</cite> for advocacy organizations.
The organization holds <cite id="1">12.0 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing significant long-term stability.
They demonstrate high donor value by spending only <cite id="1">$0.07 per $1 raised</cite>, far below typical industry benchmarks.

strengths_deep_dive: Fight CRC demonstrates a high level of fiscal responsibility by keeping administrative costs low while scaling its impact. Their <cite id="2">85.2% program expense ratio</cite> ensures that the vast majority of every dollar donated directly supports <cite id="8">research advocacy and patient education</cite>. This efficiency is bolstered by a <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> that provides rigorous oversight of the organization's <cite id="2">$3,060,301 in annual revenue</cite>. Furthermore, their <cite id="2">3-year revenue growth of 10.9%</cite> indicates a healthy expansion of their donor base and organizational capacity.
rich_narrative.summary: Fight Colorectal Cancer (Fight CRC) operates as a leading national advocacy organization dedicated to empowering survivors and advancing medical research. The organization reported <cite id="2">$3,060,301 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year, reflecting a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 10.9%</cite>. Their work focuses on a multi-pronged approach including <cite id="8">advocacy, research, and patient support</cite>. Unlike charities focused on direct clinical care, Fight CRC emphasizes <cite id="8">capacity building and policy change</cite> to create systemic impact. The organization maintains a lean operation, spending only <cite id="1">$0.07 to raise every $1</cite> in public support. This efficiency allows them to direct <cite id="2">83.4% of total expenses</cite> toward their core mission. They also maintain a highly resilient financial position with <cite id="1">12.0 months of working capital</cite> in reserve. This stability ensures they can sustain long-term research initiatives and advocacy campaigns even during economic fluctuations. While their primary operations are based in the United States, they provide <cite id="8">balanced information on cancer care</cite> to a global community of patients and caregivers.
